Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Or Pending Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, Brogan Keener-Shelby.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

James Ghilotti, Esq. appeared with petitioner Erin Shelby, who was present via CourtCall as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Brogan Keener-Shelby, who was not present for this morning's proceedings.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Ms. Shelby Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed August 24, 2015.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the Compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $8,955.20.
Attorney Fees - $1,687.62.
Costs - $2,204.70.

The net balance of $5,062.88 shall be deposited into a blocked account with the Bank of Stockton, Carson Oaks Branch, located at 6808 Pacific Avenue, Stockton, CA 95207. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of the Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to October 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for a Progress Report on the Deposit of Money into a Blocked Account.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)
